column1: # FireISP 5.0 — OWASP ZAP Passive-Scan Rule Overrides
# Format: <rule-id> TAB <IGNORE|WARN|FAIL> TAB (<optional description>)
# Reference: https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/alerts/
# 10035 — Strict-Transport-Security Not Set
# Rationale: HSTS is enforced at the reverse-proxy (Nginx) level; the Express
# app is never accessed directly by end-users in production.
# 10063 — Permissions-Policy Header Not Set
# Rationale: The Permissions-Policy header is an optional hardening header;
# helmet@8 does not set it by default. It has no security impact for an API-only
# backend. Track for a future helmet upgrade.
# 10096 — Timestamp Disclosure
# Rationale: created_at / updated_at timestamps in API responses are expected
# and documented in OpenAPI. Disclosing record timestamps is not a vulnerability.
# 10021 — X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing
# Rationale: helmet sets nosniff automatically; ZAP may flag the /health
# endpoint which responds with JSON (not HTML). Helmet is verified in unit tests.
# 90003 — Sub Resource Integrity Attribute Missing
# Rationale: FireISP serves self-hosted bundled assets from Vite; SRI is
# relevant only for CDN-hosted third-party scripts, not self-hosted bundles.

column3: (Self-hosted Vite bundle; no CDN third-party scripts)

column1: # 10110 — Dangerous JS Functions
# Rationale: ZAP flags eval() usage in bundled third-party libraries (e.g.
# regenerator-runtime). These are reviewed via SBOM + Trivy (P1.5); they are
# not in application code.

column3: (Third-party bundle internals; covered by Trivy/SBOM scan)
